```py
                if has_token_api and has_proc_resolve:
                    if target_pid_tainted or not tainted_calls:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL)
                        _record_sites(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL, TOKEN_SIG_APIS)
                if beh.get("token_offset_writes") and has_proc_resolve:
                    if target_pid_tainted or not tainted_calls:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL)
                # New: EPROCESS semantic-map writes classified as TOKEN_STEAL
                if any(w.get("primitive") == "TOKEN_STEAL"
                        for w in beh.get("eprocess_writes", [])):
                    if has_proc_resolve and (target_pid_tainted or not tainted_calls):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL)

                # 3. TOKEN MODIFY (EDR downgrade): token modify APIs + process resolve
                has_token_modify = close_apis & {
                    "ZwSetInformationToken", "NtSetInformationToken",
                    "ZwAdjustPrivilegesToken", "NtAdjustPrivilegesToken"}
                has_token_open = close_apis & {
                    "ZwOpenProcessToken", "ZwOpenProcessTokenEx",
                    "NtOpenProcessToken", "NtOpenProcessTokenEx",
                    "PsReferencePrimaryToken"}
                if has_token_modify and has_proc_resolve and has_token_open:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE)
                elif has_token_modify and has_proc_resolve:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_MODIFY)

                # 4. PPL BYPASS: semantic EPROCESS.Protection write +
                # process resolve + target PID tainted
                ppl_hits = (beh.get("ppl_byte_writes") or
                            [w for w in beh.get("eprocess_writes", [])
                              if w.get("primitive") == "PPL_BYPASS"])
                if ppl_hits and has_proc_resolve:
                    if target_pid_tainted or not tainted_calls:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS)
                # CI downgrade (SignatureLevel / SectionSignatureLevel) —
                # treat as DSE-disable class since it weakens code integrity.
                if any(w.get("primitive") == "CI_DOWNGRADE"
                        for w in beh.get("eprocess_writes", [])):
                    if has_proc_resolve and (target_pid_tainted or not tainted_calls):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_DSE_DISABLE)

                # 5. CALLBACK REMOVAL
                CB_SIG_APIS = {"PsRemoveCreateThreadNotifyRoutine",
                                "PsRemoveLoadImageNotifyRoutine",
                                "CmUnRegisterCallback", "ObUnRegisterCallbacks"}
                if close_apis & CB_SIG_APIS:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE)
                    _record_sites(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE, CB_SIG_APIS)
                # PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine can remove (Remove=TRUE)
                if "PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine" in close_apis and has_proc_resolve:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE)
                    _record_sites(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE,
                                   {"PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine"})

                # 6. ETW DISABLE
                ETW_SIG_APIS = {"NtTraceControl", "ZwTraceControl",
                                 "EtwUnregister"}
                if close_apis & ETW_SIG_APIS:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE)
                    _record_sites(self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE, ETW_SIG_APIS)

                # 7. DSE DISABLE: MmGetSystemRoutineAddress + memory write
                #    MmGet* is often wrapped in a helper — allow any depth
                #    here; write primitive must still be close.
                if "MmGetSystemRoutineAddress" in any_apis:
                    has_mem_write = close_apis & {
                        "MmCopyVirtualMemory", "ZwWriteVirtualMemory",
                        "NtWriteVirtualMemory"}
                    has_inline_write = any(op.get("type") in ("MOV_MEM", "WRITE_MEM")
                                           for op in beh.get("inline_ops", []))
                    has_cr0 = "__writecr0" in close_apis
                    if has_mem_write or has_inline_write or has_cr0:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_DSE_DISABLE)

                # 8. PROCESS ATTACH
                PA_SIG_APIS = {"KeAttachProcess", "KeStackAttachProcess"}
                if close_apis & PA_SIG_APIS:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_PROCESS_ATTACH)
                    _record_sites(self.PRIM_PROCESS_ATTACH, PA_SIG_APIS)

                # 9. THREAD INJECTION
                TI_SIG_APIS = {"RtlCreateUserThread", "ZwCreateThreadEx",
                                "NtCreateThreadEx"}
                if close_apis & TI_SIG_APIS:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_THREAD_INJECT)
                    _record_sites(self.PRIM_THREAD_INJECT, TI_SIG_APIS)
                # Keep a reference available to pre-existing code using `api_names`
                api_names = close_apis

                # 10. Memory primitives. Require user-tainted arg on the
                # *address* parameter (else we're mapping a fixed kernel
                # address on behalf of the driver's own bookkeeping).
                # Arg 0 of MmMapIoSpace = PhysicalAddress.
                direct_mem_apis = [ac for ac in beh["api_calls"]
                                    if ac["name"] in ("MmMapIoSpace", "MmMapLockedPages",
                                                       "MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache")
                                    and ac.get("depth", 0) <= 1]
                if direct_mem_apis:
                    # PHYS_RW only when the PhysicalAddress arg is
                    # user-controlled — pinning a fixed kernel page is
                    # not arbitrary.
                    if (_user_controlled("MmMapIoSpace", {0, 1}) or
                            _user_controlled("MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache",
                                              {0, 1})):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_PHYS_RW)
                        _record_sites(self.PRIM_PHYS_RW,
                                       {"MmMapIoSpace", "MmMapLockedPages",
                                        "MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache"})

                # Zw/NtWriteVirtualMemory args: Handle, BaseAddress,
                # Buffer, BufferLength, WriteSize. Arg 1 (BaseAddress)
                # being tainted ⇒ user picks where to write.
                direct_rw = [ac for ac in beh["api_calls"]
                              if ac["name"] in ("ZwWriteVirtualMemory", "NtWriteVirtualMemory",
                                                "MmCopyVirtualMemory")
                              and ac.get("depth", 0) <= 1]
                if direct_rw:
                    # Address arg = idx 1 (BaseAddress) for Zw/NtWriteVM,
                    # idx 2 (TargetAddress) for MmCopyVirtualMemory.
                    if (_user_controlled("ZwWriteVirtualMemory", {1, 2}) or
                            _user_controlled("NtWriteVirtualMemory", {1, 2}) or
                            _user_controlled("MmCopyVirtualMemory", {2, 3})):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE)
                        _record_sites(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE,
                                       {"ZwWriteVirtualMemory",
                                        "NtWriteVirtualMemory",
                                        "MmCopyVirtualMemory"})

                direct_read = [ac for ac in beh["api_calls"]
                                if ac["name"] in ("ZwReadVirtualMemory", "NtReadVirtualMemory")
                                and ac.get("depth", 0) <= 1]
                if direct_read:
                    if (_user_controlled("ZwReadVirtualMemory", {1}) or
                            _user_controlled("NtReadVirtualMemory", {1})):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_READ)
                        _record_sites(self.PRIM_ARB_READ,
                                       {"ZwReadVirtualMemory",
                                        "NtReadVirtualMemory"})

                # 11. MSR — tag ARBITRARY only when MSR id is user-controlled
                # (inline wrmsr against an immediate-loaded ECX is not arb).
                if any_apis & {"__readmsr", "__writemsr", "_readmsr", "_writemsr"}:
                    if (_user_controlled("__writemsr", {0}) or
                            _user_controlled("__readmsr", {0})):
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_MSR_RW)

                # 12. From risk factors (catch-all for patterns detected earlier)
                for rf in risk_factors:
                    if "TOKEN STEAL" in rf:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL)
                    elif "PPL BYPASS" in rf:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS)
                    elif "CALLBACK REMOVAL" in rf or "CALLBACK CONTROL" in rf:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE)
                    elif "ETW DISABLE" in rf:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE)
                    elif "EDR TOKEN DOWNGRADE" in rf:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE)

                # 13. ONLY add generic process-control if no specific process
                #     primitive was identified AND it has PsLookupProcessByProcessId
                process_prims = {self.PRIM_PROCESS_KILL, self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL,
                                 self.PRIM_TOKEN_MODIFY, self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS,
                                 self.PRIM_PROCESS_ATTACH, self.PRIM_THREAD_INJECT,
                                 self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE, self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE}
                if not (primitives & process_prims) and has_proc_resolve:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_PROCESS_CTRL)

            elif handler_va is not None:
                # Fallback: direct IAT scan (no behavior data)
                rva = handler_va - image_base
                data = self.pe.get_bytes_at_rva(rva, 8192)
                if data:
                    insns = self.dis.disassemble_function(data, handler_va, max_insns=500)
                    if insns:
                        call_targets = self.dis.find_all_call_targets(insns)
                        for _, target in call_targets:
                            fn = self.pe.iat_map.get(target, "")
                            if fn in self._API_TO_PRIM:
                                primitives.add(self._API_TO_PRIM[fn])

            # ── Analyze existing findings for this IOCTL ──────────────────
            for f in findings_by_code.get(code, []):
                title_lower = f.title.lower() if f.title else ""
                desc_lower = f.description.lower() if f.description else ""

                # METHOD_NEITHER without probe → arb read + arb write
                if "method_neither" in title_lower or "method_neither" in desc_lower:
                    if "probe" in title_lower or "probe" in desc_lower:
                        if "no probe" in desc_lower or "without probe" in desc_lower or "missing" in desc_lower:
                            primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_READ)
                            primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE)
                    elif "neither" in title_lower:
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_READ)
                        primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE)

                if "arbitrary write" in title_lower or "arbitrary write" in desc_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE)
                if "info leak" in title_lower or "information leak" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_INFO_LEAK)
                if "kernel info leak" in title_lower or "kaslr" in desc_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_INFO_LEAK)
                if "ppl" in title_lower and "bypass" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS)
                if "token" in title_lower and "steal" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL)
                if "token" in title_lower and "downgrade" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE)
                if "callback" in title_lower and "removal" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE)
                if "etw" in title_lower and "disabl" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE)
                if "dse" in title_lower and ("disabl" in title_lower or "bypass" in title_lower):
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_DSE_DISABLE)
                if "integer overflow" in title_lower and "alloc" in desc_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_POOL_OVERFLOW)
                if "stack overflow" in title_lower or "stack buffer" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_CODE_EXEC)
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_DOS)
                if "double fetch" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE)
                if "taint" in title_lower and "user" in title_lower:
                    primitives.add(self.PRIM_DOS)

            if primitives:
                self.ioctl_primitives[code] = sorted(primitives)
                decoded = decode_ioctl(code)

                # Determine severity based on primitive types
                critical_prims = {self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE, self.PRIM_CODE_EXEC,
                                  self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL, self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS,
                                  self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE, self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE,
                                  self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE, self.PRIM_DSE_DISABLE,
                                  self.PRIM_PROCESS_KILL}
                high_prims = {self.PRIM_ARB_READ, self.PRIM_PHYS_RW, self.PRIM_MSR_RW,
                              self.PRIM_PROCESS_CTRL, self.PRIM_PROCESS_ATTACH,
                              self.PRIM_THREAD_INJECT, self.PRIM_TOKEN_MODIFY}
                if primitives & critical_prims:
                    sev = Severity.CRITICAL
                elif primitives & high_prims:
                    sev = Severity.HIGH
                else:
                    sev = Severity.MEDIUM

                prim_list = ", ".join(sorted(primitives))
                self.findings.append(Finding(
                    title=f"Exploit primitives for IOCTL {decoded['code']}: {prim_list}",
                    severity=sev,
                    description=(
                        f"IOCTL {decoded['code']} ({decoded['method_name']}) provides "
                        f"the following exploit primitives: {prim_list}. "
                        f"Handler at 0x{handler_va:X}." if handler_va else
                        f"IOCTL {decoded['code']} ({decoded['method_name']}) provides "
                        f"the following exploit primitives: {prim_list}."
                    ),
                    location=f"0x{handler_va:X}" if handler_va else "Unknown handler",
                    ioctl_code=code,
                    details={
                        "ioctl": decoded["code"],
                        "primitives": sorted(primitives),
                        "handler": f"0x{handler_va:X}" if handler_va else "unknown",
                    },
                ))

        # ── Build reverse shared-site index ───────────────────────────────
        # (primitive, call_va) → [codes reaching that exact call site].
        # Two IOCTLs present under the same key → they converge on a
        # single underlying implementation — one primitive, multiple
        # entry points.
        shared: Dict[tuple, List[int]] = {}
        for code, prim_map in self.ioctl_primitive_sites.items():
            for prim, vas in prim_map.items():
                for va in vas:
                    shared.setdefault((prim, va), []).append(code)
        # Keep only sites genuinely shared (>1 code).
        self.primitive_shared_sites = {
            k: sorted(set(v)) for k, v in shared.items() if len(set(v)) > 1
        }

        # ── Thin-wrapper detection ────────────────────────────────────────
        self.ioctl_thin_wrapper_of = self._detect_thin_wrappers()

        # ── Overall summary finding ───────────────────────────────────────
        if self.ioctl_primitives:
            summary_lines = []
            all_prims: set = set()
            for code, prims in sorted(self.ioctl_primitives.items()):
                decoded = decode_ioctl(code)
                summary_lines.append(f"  {decoded['code']}: {', '.join(prims)}")
                all_prims.update(prims)

            critical_prims = {self.PRIM_ARB_WRITE, self.PRIM_CODE_EXEC,
                              self.PRIM_TOKEN_STEAL, self.PRIM_PPL_BYPASS,
                              self.PRIM_CALLBACK_REMOVE, self.PRIM_ETW_DISABLE,
                              self.PRIM_EDR_DOWNGRADE, self.PRIM_DSE_DISABLE,
                              self.PRIM_PROCESS_KILL}
            if all_prims & critical_prims:
                overall_sev = Severity.CRITICAL
            else:
                overall_sev = Severity.HIGH

            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title="Exploit Primitive Summary",
                severity=overall_sev,
                description=(
                    f"Across {len(self.ioctl_primitives)} IOCTL(s), the driver exposes "
                    f"{len(all_prims)} distinct exploit primitive type(s):\n"
                    + "\n".join(summary_lines)
                ),
                location="All IOCTL handlers",
                details={
                    "total_ioctls": str(len(self.ioctl_primitives)),
                    "all_primitives": sorted(all_prims),
                    "per_ioctl": {
                        f"0x{c:08X}": p for c, p in self.ioctl_primitives.items()
                    },
                },
            ))

    def _detect_thin_wrappers(self) -> Dict[int, int]:
        """Identify handlers that are thin wrappers of another handler.

        A thin wrapper A → B has:
          * Short body (≤ 20 instructions up to ret/jmp-out)
          * Exactly one unique internal `call` target
          * That target's VA matches another IOCTL's handler VA
          * No direct IAT calls in its own body

        Returns {wrapper_code: target_code}. Multiple IOCTLs can wrap
        the same target. When A wraps B but B itself wraps C, we
        transitively resolve A → C.
        """
        import capstone.x86_const as _x86c
        image_base = self.pe.pe.OPTIONAL_HEADER.ImageBase
        handler_to_code: Dict[int, int] = {}
        for code in self.ioctl_codes:
            hva = self._get_handler_va(code)
            if hva is not None:
                # Prefer a code that is not already tagged as a wrapper —
                # but since we're about to build that set, just keep the
                # first-seen code per VA.
                handler_to_code.setdefault(hva, code)

        wrappers: Dict[int, int] = {}
        for code, hva in ((c, self._get_handler_va(c)) for c in self.ioctl_codes):
            if hva is None:
                continue
            rva = hva - image_base
            data = self.pe.get_bytes_at_rva(rva, 256)
            if not data:
                continue
            try:
                insns = self.dis.disassemble_function(data, hva, max_insns=25)
            except Exception:
                continue
            if not insns or len(insns) > 22:
                continue
            # Must end in ret/jmp/tail-call
            last = insns[-1]
            if last.mnemonic not in ("ret", "retn", "jmp", "int3"):
                continue

            call_targets: set = set()
            iat_calls = 0
            for ins in insns:
                if ins.mnemonic != "call" or not ins.operands:
                    continue
                op = ins.operands[0]
                if op.type == _x86c.X86_OP_IMM:
                    tgt = op.imm
                elif (op.type == _x86c.X86_OP_MEM and
                        op.mem.base == _x86c.X86_REG_RIP and
                        op.mem.index == 0):
                    tgt = ins.address + ins.size + op.mem.disp
                else:
                    continue
                if tgt in self.pe.iat_map:
                    iat_calls += 1
                else:
                    call_targets.add(tgt)
            # Thin wrapper: no IAT calls + exactly one internal target
            # that matches another handler
            if iat_calls != 0 or len(call_targets) != 1:
                continue
            target_va = next(iter(call_targets))
            if target_va == hva:
                continue  # self-reference
            target_code = handler_to_code.get(target_va)
            if target_code is None or target_code == code:
                continue
            wrappers[code] = target_code

        # Transitively resolve chains (A wraps B wraps C → A wraps C)
        resolved: Dict[int, int] = {}
        for code, tgt in wrappers.items():
            seen = {code}
            while tgt in wrappers and tgt not in seen:
                seen.add(tgt)
                tgt = wrappers[tgt]
            resolved[code] = tgt
        return resolved

    def find_rop_gadgets(self):
        """
        Find usable ROP/JOP gadgets inside the driver binary.
        Scans executable sections backward from every RET/JMP REG instruction.
        Classifies gadgets by utility for exploitation.
        """
        GADGET_MAX_INSNS = 5   # max instructions per gadget
        GADGET_MAX_BYTES = 20  # max bytes to scan backward
        code_sections = self.pe.get_code_sections()
        if not code_sections:
            return

        md = capstone.Cs(capstone.CS_ARCH_X86,
                         capstone.CS_MODE_64 if self.pe.is_64bit else capstone.CS_MODE_32)
        md.detail = True

        # Gadget endings
        RET_BYTES = [b"\xc3", b"\xc2"]  # ret, ret imm16
        gadgets_by_type: Dict[str, List[dict]] = {
            "stack-pivot": [], "reg-control": [], "memory-read": [],
            "memory-write": [], "privilege-op": [], "syscall": [],
            "jmp-reg": [], "call-reg": [], "misc": [],
        }
        seen_gadget_bytes: set = set()

        for sec_va, sec_data in code_sections:
            data_len = len(sec_data)
            # Find all RET positions
            ret_positions = []
            for i in range(data_len):
                if sec_data[i:i+1] == b"\xc3":
                    ret_positions.append(i)
                elif sec_data[i:i+1] == b"\xc2" and i + 2 < data_len:
                    ret_positions.append(i)

            for ret_pos in ret_positions:
                # Scan backward
                for back in range(1, GADGET_MAX_BYTES + 1):
                    start = ret_pos - back
                    if start < 0:
                        break
                    chunk = sec_data[start:ret_pos + 1]
                    # Skip if we've seen these exact bytes
                    if chunk in seen_gadget_bytes:
                        continue

                    va = sec_va + start
                    insns = list(md.disasm(chunk, va))
                    if not insns:
                        continue
                    # Must end at ret
                    last = insns[-1]
                    if last.mnemonic not in ("ret", "retn"):
                        continue
                    if len(insns) < 2 or len(insns) > GADGET_MAX_INSNS + 1:
                        continue
                    # Must decode exactly to end of chunk
                    decoded_end = insns[-1].address + insns[-1].size
                    expected_end = va + len(chunk)
                    if decoded_end != expected_end:
                        continue

                    seen_gadget_bytes.add(chunk)
                    asm_str = " ; ".join(f"{i.mnemonic} {i.op_str}".strip()
                                         for i in insns)
                    gadget_entry = {
                        "va": va,
                        "asm": asm_str,
                        "bytes": chunk.hex(),
                        "insn_count": len(insns),
                    }

                    # Classify
                    gtype = self._classify_gadget(insns[:-1], asm_str)
                    gadgets_by_type[gtype].append(gadget_entry)

        # Also find JMP REG / CALL REG gadgets (JOP)
        for sec_va, sec_data in code_sections:
            for i in range(len(sec_data) - 1):
                # jmp rax=FF E0, jmp rcx=FF E1, etc.
                if sec_data[i] == 0xFF and sec_data[i+1] in (0xE0, 0xE1, 0xE2, 0xE3,
                                                              0xE4, 0xE5, 0xE6, 0xE7):
                    va = sec_va + i
                    for back in range(1, GADGET_MAX_BYTES):
                        s = i - back
                        if s < 0:
                            break
                        chunk = sec_data[s:i + 2]
                        insns = list(md.disasm(chunk, sec_va + s))
                        if not insns or len(insns) < 2 or len(insns) > GADGET_MAX_INSNS + 1:
                            continue
                        last = insns[-1]
                        if last.mnemonic != "jmp":
                            continue
                        if chunk in seen_gadget_bytes:
                            continue
                        seen_gadget_bytes.add(chunk)
                        asm_str = " ; ".join(f"{i.mnemonic} {i.op_str}".strip()
                                             for i in insns)
                        gadgets_by_type["jmp-reg"].append({
                            "va": sec_va + s, "asm": asm_str,
                            "bytes": chunk.hex(), "insn_count": len(insns),
                        })

        # Store and report
        total = sum(len(v) for v in gadgets_by_type.values())
        self.rop_gadgets = gadgets_by_type

        if total == 0:
            return

        # Report top gadgets per type
        useful_types = {k: v for k, v in gadgets_by_type.items() if v}
        summary_parts = []
        for gtype, glist in sorted(useful_types.items(), key=lambda x: -len(x[1])):
            summary_parts.append(f"{gtype}: {len(glist)}")

        self.findings.append(Finding(
            title=f"ROP/JOP gadgets found: {total} total ({', '.join(summary_parts)})",
            severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
            description=f"Found {total} usable ROP/JOP gadgets in executable sections. "
                        f"Since many drivers lack ASLR, these gadgets are at predictable "
                        f"addresses and can be chained for kernel exploitation.\n\n"
                        f"Top gadgets by type:\n" +
                        self._format_top_gadgets(useful_types),
            location="Executable sections",
            details={"total_gadgets": str(total),
                     "by_type": {k: len(v) for k, v in useful_types.items()}},
        ))

        # Report privilege-ops separately (most dangerous)
        for gtype in ("privilege-op", "memory-write", "stack-pivot"):
            if gadgets_by_type.get(gtype):
                best = gadgets_by_type[gtype][:5]
                for g in best:
                    self.findings.append(Finding(
                        title=f"ROP gadget [{gtype}]: {g['asm']}",
                        severity=Severity.HIGH if gtype == "privilege-op" else Severity.MEDIUM,
                        description=f"Gadget at 0x{g['va']:X} ({g['insn_count']} insns). "
                                    f"Type: {gtype}. Bytes: {g['bytes']}",
                        location=f"0x{g['va']:X}",
                        details={"type": gtype, "asm": g["asm"], "bytes": g["bytes"]},
                    ))

    def _classify_gadget(self, insns: list, asm_str: str) -> str:
        """Classify a gadget by its exploitation utility."""
        asm_lower = asm_str.lower()
        # Privilege operations
        if any(m in asm_lower for m in ("mov cr", "wrmsr", "writemsr", "wbinvd",
                                         "invd", "swapgs", "sysret", "iretq")):
            return "privilege-op"
        # Stack pivot
        if any(m in asm_lower for m in ("xchg", "mov rsp", "mov esp",
                                         "leave", "pop rsp", "pop esp")):
            return "stack-pivot"
        # Memory write
        for insn in insns:
            if insn.mnemonic == "mov" and len(insn.operands) == 2:
                if insn.operands[0].type == x86c.X86_OP_MEM:
                    return "memory-write"
        # Memory read
        for insn in insns:
            if insn.mnemonic in ("mov", "movzx", "movsx") and len(insn.operands) == 2:
                if insn.operands[1].type == x86c.X86_OP_MEM:
                    return "memory-read"
        # Register control (pop reg)
        if "pop " in asm_lower:
            return "reg-control"
        # Syscall
        if "syscall" in asm_lower or "int " in asm_lower:
            return "syscall"
        return "misc"

    def _format_top_gadgets(self, by_type: Dict[str, List[dict]]) -> str:
        """Format top 3 gadgets per type for display."""
        lines = []
        for gtype in ("privilege-op", "stack-pivot", "memory-write",
                       "memory-read", "reg-control", "jmp-reg"):
            glist = by_type.get(gtype, [])
            if not glist:
                continue
            lines.append(f"  [{gtype}] ({len(glist)} found):")
            for g in glist[:3]:
                lines.append(f"    0x{g['va']:X}: {g['asm']}")
        return "\n".join(lines)

    def build_exploit_chains(self):
        """
        Given discovered primitives, taint paths, and gadgets, automatically
        build multi-step exploit strategies (chains).

        Common chains:
        1. info-leak → KASLR defeat → arb-write → token overwrite → SYSTEM
        2. arb-read (kernel base) → arb-write (token) → spawn shell
        3. phys-rw → map kernel page → overwrite code → execute
        4. msr-rw → disable SMEP → user shellcode exec
        5. process-control → attach to SYSTEM → steal token
        """
        all_prims = {}
        for code, prims in self.ioctl_primitives.items():
            for p in prims:
                if p not in all_prims:
                    all_prims[p] = []
                all_prims[p].append(code)

        if not all_prims:
            return

        chains: List[dict] = []
        has_gadgets = hasattr(self, "rop_gadgets") and self.rop_gadgets

        # ── Chain 1: Token Steal via Arbitrary R/W ─────────────────────
        if "arbitrary-read" in all_prims and "arbitrary-write" in all_prims:
            chains.append({
                "name": "Token Steal via Arbitrary R/W",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use arb-read (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['arbitrary-read'])}) "
                    f"to leak nt!PsInitialSystemProcess address",
                    "2. Walk EPROCESS.ActiveProcessLinks to find current process",
                    f"3. Use arb-read to read SYSTEM token from EPROCESS+0x4B8",
                    f"4. Use arb-write (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['arbitrary-write'])}) "
                    f"to overwrite current process token with SYSTEM token",
                    "5. Spawn cmd.exe → NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM",
                ],
                "primitives": ["arbitrary-read", "arbitrary-write"],
                "difficulty": "LOW — fully deterministic, no spraying needed",
            })

        # ── Chain 2: KASLR Bypass + Arb Write ─────────────────────────
        if "info-leak" in all_prims and "arbitrary-write" in all_prims:
            chains.append({
                "name": "KASLR Defeat + Token Overwrite",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use info-leak (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['info-leak'])}) "
                    f"to leak kernel pointer, defeat KASLR",
                    "2. Calculate nt base from leaked pointer",
                    "3. Read PsInitialSystemProcess from nt+offset",
                    f"4. Use arb-write (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['arbitrary-write'])}) "
                    f"to copy SYSTEM token to current process",
                    "5. Spawn elevated shell",
                ],
                "primitives": ["info-leak", "arbitrary-write"],
                "difficulty": "LOW — info leak provides reliable KASLR defeat",
            })

        # ── Chain 3: Physical Memory R/W → Full Compromise ────────────
        if "physical-rw" in all_prims:
            chains.append({
                "name": "Physical Memory Attack",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use phys-rw (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['physical-rw'])}) "
                    f"to scan physical memory for EPROCESS signatures",
                    "2. Locate current process EPROCESS in physical memory",
                    "3. Overwrite Token field with SYSTEM process token",
                    "4. Alternative: map ntoskrnl .text, patch code inline",
                    "5. Spawn SYSTEM shell",
                ],
                "primitives": ["physical-rw"],
                "difficulty": "MEDIUM — requires physical-to-virtual translation",
            })

        # ── Chain 4: MSR Write → Disable SMEP → Shellcode ────────────
        if "msr-rw" in all_prims:
            steps = [
                f"1. Use msr-rw (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['msr-rw'])}) "
                f"to read IA32_LSTAR (MSR 0xC0000082) — get kernel base",
                "2. Write IA32_LSTAR to redirect syscall to user shellcode, OR",
                "3. Read CR4 via MSR, clear SMEP bit (bit 20), write back",
                "4. Allocate user-mode page, place kernel shellcode",
                "5. Trigger execution (call gate / APC / timer)",
            ]
            if has_gadgets and self.rop_gadgets.get("privilege-op"):
                steps.append(f"   ROP gadget available: "
                             f"{self.rop_gadgets['privilege-op'][0]['asm']}")
            chains.append({
                "name": "MSR-based SMEP/SMAP Bypass",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": steps,
                "primitives": ["msr-rw"],
                "difficulty": "MEDIUM — MSR writes may trigger PatchGuard",
            })

        # ── Chain 5: Token Steal (direct) ─────────────────────────────
        if "token-steal" in all_prims:
            steal_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["token-steal"],
                                              "token steal")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Direct Token Steal → SYSTEM",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use token-steal IOCTL ({steal_code}) with our PID",
                    "2. Driver copies SYSTEM EPROCESS.Token to our process",
                    "3. Spawn cmd.exe → NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM",
                ],
                "primitives": ["token-steal"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL gives SYSTEM token",
            })

        # ── Chain 5b: Process Attach → Token Steal ─────────────────────
        if "process-attach" in all_prims and "token-steal" not in all_prims:
            attach_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["process-attach"],
                                               "process attach")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Process Attach + Token Copy",
                "severity": Severity.HIGH,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use attach IOCTL ({attach_code}) to attach to SYSTEM (PID 4)",
                    "2. While attached, read SYSTEM token from EPROCESS",
                    "3. Copy token to current process EPROCESS",
                    "4. Detach from SYSTEM process",
                    "5. Current process now runs as SYSTEM",
                ],
                "primitives": ["process-attach"],
                "difficulty": "LOW — if driver exposes KeAttachProcess",
            })

        # ── Chain 5c: Process Kill ─────────────────────────────────────
        if "process-kill" in all_prims:
            kill_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["process-kill"],
                                             "process kill")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Process Kill (terminate any process)",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use kill IOCTL ({kill_code}) with target PID",
                    "2. Driver calls ZwTerminateProcess in kernel context",
                    "3. Bypasses usermode protection — kills PPL/protected processes",
                ],
                "primitives": ["process-kill"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL kills any process",
            })

        # ── Chain 5d: Fallback generic process control ─────────────────
        if ("process-control" in all_prims and "token-steal" not in all_prims
                and "process-attach" not in all_prims
                and "process-kill" not in all_prims):
            ctrl_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["process-control"],
                                             "process lookup", "process access")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Process Control primitive",
                "severity": Severity.HIGH,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use process-control IOCTL ({ctrl_code}) to resolve target process",
                    "2. Driver can access EPROCESS of arbitrary processes",
                    "3. Combine with memory write for token steal / PPL bypass",
                ],
                "primitives": ["process-control"],
                "difficulty": "MEDIUM — needs second primitive for exploitation",
            })

        # ── Chain 6: PPL Bypass → Dump LSASS ─────────────────────────
        if "ppl-bypass" in all_prims:
            chains.append({
                "name": "PPL Bypass → Credential Dump",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use ppl-bypass (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['ppl-bypass'])}) "
                    f"to remove PPL from lsass.exe (PID from tasklist)",
                    "2. lsass.exe is now unprotected",
                    "3. Use MiniDumpWriteDump or ProcDump to dump lsass memory",
                    "4. Extract credentials with Mimikatz offline",
                ],
                "primitives": ["ppl-bypass"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL removes PPL",
            })

        # ── Chain 7: Pool Overflow → Code Execution ──────────────────
        if "pool-overflow" in all_prims:
            chain = {
                "name": "Pool Overflow → Kernel Code Execution",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Spray kernel pool with target objects (e.g., named pipes)",
                    f"2. Free one allocation to create hole",
                    f"3. Use pool-overflow (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['pool-overflow'])}) "
                    f"to overflow into adjacent object",
                    "4. Corrupt object vtable/function pointer",
                    "5. Trigger corrupted object → hijack control flow",
                ],
                "primitives": ["pool-overflow"],
                "difficulty": "HIGH — requires heap feng shui",
            }
            if has_gadgets and self.rop_gadgets.get("stack-pivot"):
                chain["steps"].append(
                    f"   Stack pivot gadget: {self.rop_gadgets['stack-pivot'][0]['asm']}")
            chains.append(chain)

        # ── Chain 8: Arb Write only (no read) → Blind Token Overwrite ─
        if "arbitrary-write" in all_prims and "arbitrary-read" not in all_prims:
            leak = ("info-leak" in all_prims or "physical-rw" in all_prims
                    or "msr-rw" in all_prims)
            chains.append({
                "name": "Blind Arbitrary Write" + (" (with leak assist)" if leak else ""),
                "severity": Severity.HIGH,
                "steps": [
                    "1. Obtain kernel base:" + (
                        " use info-leak/phys-rw/msr IOCTL" if leak
                        else " NtQuerySystemInformation or EnumDeviceDrivers (user-mode)"),
                    "2. Calculate PsInitialSystemProcess address",
                    f"3. Use arb-write (IOCTL {self._fmt_codes(all_prims['arbitrary-write'])}) "
                    f"to overwrite current process token",
                    "4. Alternatively: overwrite HalDispatchTable+0x8 for code exec",
                    "5. Spawn SYSTEM shell",
                ],
                "primitives": ["arbitrary-write"] + (["info-leak"] if leak else []),
                "difficulty": "MEDIUM — blind write requires known offsets",
            })

        # ── Chain 9: Callback Removal → Blind EDR ──────────────────
        if "callback-removal" in all_prims:
            cb_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["callback-removal"],
                                           "callback removal")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Kernel Callback Removal → Blind EDR",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use callback-removal IOCTL ({cb_code})",
                    "2. Driver removes PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine callbacks",
                    "3. Driver removes ObRegisterCallbacks (handle protection)",
                    "4. EDR loses process/image/registry monitoring",
                    "5. Execute malware undetected by kernel-level monitoring",
                ],
                "primitives": ["callback-removal"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL blinds EDR",
            })

        # ── Chain 10: ETW Disable → Blind Telemetry ───────────────────
        if "etw-disable" in all_prims:
            etw_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["etw-disable"],
                                            "etw disable")
            chains.append({
                "name": "ETW Provider Disable → Blind Telemetry",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use etw-disable IOCTL ({etw_code})",
                    "2. Driver patches EtwEventWrite or clears provider enable mask",
                    "3. Microsoft-Windows-Threat-Intelligence (EtwTi) goes dark",
                    "4. EDR syscall monitoring, memory tracking disabled",
                    "5. Execute attacks invisible to ETW-based detection",
                ],
                "primitives": ["etw-disable"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL disables ETW",
            })

        # ── Chain 11: EDR Token Downgrade ─────────────────────────────
        if "edr-token-downgrade" in all_prims:
            edr_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["edr-token-downgrade"],
                                            "edr token downgrade", "token modify")
            chains.append({
                "name": "EDR Token Downgrade → Neuter Security",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use token-downgrade IOCTL ({edr_code}) with EDR PID",
                    "2. Driver opens EDR process token, sets integrity to Untrusted",
                    "3. Driver strips SeDebugPrivilege and all dangerous privileges",
                    "4. EDR process still runs but cannot access other processes",
                    "5. No tamper-protection alert (process not killed)",
                ],
                "primitives": ["edr-token-downgrade"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — single IOCTL neuters EDR",
            })

        # ── Chain 12: DSE Disable → Load Unsigned Driver ──────────────
        if "dse-disable" in all_prims:
            dse_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["dse-disable"],
                                            "runtime resolve")
            chains.append({
                "name": "DSE Disable → Load Unsigned Driver (VBS off)",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use DSE-disable IOCTL ({dse_code})",
                    "2. Driver resolves CI!g_CiOptions via MmGetSystemRoutineAddress",
                    "3. Patches g_CiOptions = 0x0000 (disable signature enforcement)",
                    "4. Load unsigned rootkit/driver via sc.exe create + start",
                    "5. Restore g_CiOptions to avoid detection",
                ],
                "primitives": ["dse-disable"],
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — requires VBS disabled (check: bcdedit)",
            })

        # ── Chain 13: Thread Injection ────────────────────────────────
        if "thread-injection" in all_prims:
            inj_code = self._best_code_for(all_prims["thread-injection"],
                                            "create thread")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Kernel Thread Injection",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": [
                    f"1. Use thread-injection IOCTL ({inj_code})",
                    "2. Driver creates thread in target process from kernel",
                    "3. Bypasses usermode injection detection (no OpenProcess from user)",
                    "4. Execute shellcode in target process context",
                ],
                "primitives": ["thread-injection"],
                "difficulty": "LOW — direct kernel thread creation",
            })

        # ── Chain 14: Full EDR Bypass Combo ───────────────────────────
        combo_prims = {"callback-removal", "etw-disable", "edr-token-downgrade"}
        found_combo = combo_prims & set(all_prims.keys())
        if len(found_combo) >= 2:
            steps = ["--- Full EDR Bypass Sequence ---"]
            step_n = 1
            if "etw-disable" in all_prims:
                etw = self._best_code_for(all_prims["etw-disable"], "etw disable")
                steps.append(f"{step_n}. Disable ETW telemetry ({etw})")
                step_n += 1
            if "callback-removal" in all_prims:
                cb = self._best_code_for(all_prims["callback-removal"], "callback removal")
                steps.append(f"{step_n}. Remove kernel callbacks ({cb})")
                step_n += 1
            if "edr-token-downgrade" in all_prims:
                edr = self._best_code_for(all_prims["edr-token-downgrade"],
                                           "edr token downgrade", "token modify")
                steps.append(f"{step_n}. Downgrade EDR process token ({edr})")
                step_n += 1
            if "process-kill" in all_prims:
                kill = self._best_code_for(all_prims["process-kill"], "process kill")
                steps.append(f"{step_n}. Kill remaining EDR processes ({kill})")
                step_n += 1
            steps.append(f"{step_n}. System is now fully unmonitored")
            chains.append({
                "name": "Full EDR Bypass (multi-IOCTL combo)",
                "severity": Severity.CRITICAL,
                "steps": steps,
                "primitives": sorted(found_combo),
                "difficulty": "TRIVIAL — sequential IOCTL calls disable all monitoring",
            })

        self.exploit_chains = chains
        self.type_chain_dataflow()

        # Generate findings
        for chain in chains:
            step_text = "\n".join(f"  {s}" for s in chain["steps"])
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title=f"Exploit Chain: {chain['name']}",
                severity=chain["severity"],
                description=f"Difficulty: {chain['difficulty']}\n"
                            f"Required primitives: {', '.join(chain['primitives'])}\n\n"
                            f"Attack steps:\n{step_text}",
                location="Exploit Strategy",
                details={
                    "chain_name": chain["name"],
                    "primitives": chain["primitives"],
                    "difficulty": chain["difficulty"],
                },
            ))

        if chains:
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title=f"Total exploit chains identified: {len(chains)}",
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL if any(
                    c["severity"] == Severity.CRITICAL for c in chains
                ) else Severity.HIGH,
                description=f"The driver's exposed primitives can be combined into "
                            f"{len(chains)} distinct exploit chain(s):\n" +
                            "\n".join(f"  - {c['name']} [{c['difficulty'].split(' —')[0]}]"
                                     for c in chains),
                location="Exploit Strategy",
                details={"chain_count": str(len(chains)),
                         "chains": [c["name"] for c in chains]},
            ))

    def _fmt_codes(self, codes: List[int]) -> str:
        """Format IOCTL code list for display — show the best one first with purpose."""
        if not codes:
            return "unknown"
        annotated = []
        for c in codes:
            purpose = self.ioctl_purposes.get(c, "")
            label = f"0x{c:08X}"
            if purpose:
                label += f" [{purpose}]"
            annotated.append(label)
        if len(annotated) <= 3:
            return ", ".join(annotated)
        return ", ".join(annotated[:3]) + f" +{len(annotated)-3} more"

    def _best_code_for(self, codes: List[int], *target_purposes: str) -> str:
        """Pick the SINGLE best IOCTL code that matches one of target_purposes.
        Falls back to first code with purpose annotation."""
        for purpose in target_purposes:
            for c in codes:
                if self.ioctl_purposes.get(c, "") == purpose:
                    return f"0x{c:08X} [{purpose}]"
        # No exact match — return first with annotation
        c = codes[0]
        purpose = self.ioctl_purposes.get(c, "")
        return f"0x{c:08X}" + (f" [{purpose}]" if purpose else "")

    def detect_state_machine(self):
        """
        Detect multi-step attack sequences where exploiting a vulnerability
        requires sending IOCTLs in a specific order.

        Analysis:
        1. Find global variables written by one IOCTL handler and read by another
        2. Detect "init → configure → trigger" patterns
        3. Build a state transition graph
        4. Identify attack sequences that unlock dangerous operations
        """
        if len(self.ioctl_codes) < 2:
            return

        image_base = self.pe.pe.OPTIONAL_HEADER.ImageBase

        # Phase 1: For each IOCTL handler, find global memory reads and writes
        # A "global" is a memory access with RIP-relative addressing or absolute address
        handler_globals: Dict[int, dict] = {}  # ioctl → {"reads": set, "writes": set}

        for code in self.ioctl_codes:
            handler_va = self._get_handler_va(code)
            if not handler_va:
                continue
            rva = handler_va - image_base
            hbytes = self.pe.get_bytes_at_rva(rva, 8192)
            if not hbytes:
                continue
            insns = self.dis.disassemble_function(hbytes, handler_va, max_insns=500)

            reads = set()
            writes = set()

            for insn in insns:
                if not insn.operands or len(insn.operands) < 2:
                    continue
                # Skip call/jmp
                if insn.mnemonic in ("call", "jmp", "je", "jne", "jz", "jnz",
                                     "jb", "ja", "jl", "jg", "js", "jns",
                                     "jbe", "jae", "jle", "jge"):
                    continue

                for oi, op in enumerate(insn.operands):
                    if op.type != x86c.X86_OP_MEM:
                        continue
                    # RIP-relative → global variable
                    if op.mem.base == x86c.X86_REG_RIP:
                        global_va = insn.address + insn.size + op.mem.disp
                        # Determine if read or write
                        if oi == 0 and insn.mnemonic in ("mov", "add", "sub", "or",
                                                          "and", "xor", "inc", "dec",
                                                          "shl", "shr"):
                            writes.add(global_va)
                        elif oi == 1 or insn.mnemonic in ("cmp", "test"):
                            reads.add(global_va)
                        elif oi == 0 and insn.mnemonic in ("cmp", "test"):
                            reads.add(global_va)

            handler_globals[code] = {"reads": reads, "writes": writes}

        # Phase 2: Find cross-handler dependencies
        # If IOCTL A writes global X and IOCTL B reads global X,
        # then B depends on A (A must run before B for the state to be set)
        transitions: List[dict] = []
        for code_a, ga in handler_globals.items():
            for code_b, gb in handler_globals.items():
                if code_a == code_b:
                    continue
                shared = ga["writes"] & gb["reads"]
                if shared:
                    transitions.append({
                        "from": code_a,
                        "to": code_b,
                        "shared_globals": sorted(shared),
                        "count": len(shared),
                    })

        if not transitions:
            return

        # Phase 3: Build state graph and find attack sequences
        # Look for chains: init → configure → trigger (dangerous operation)
        graph: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)
        for t in transitions:
            graph[t["from"]].append(t["to"])

        # Find which IOCTLs have dangerous operations (from primitives)
        dangerous_ioctls = set()
        for code, prims in self.ioctl_primitives.items():
            if any(p in ("arbitrary-write", "arbitrary-read", "code-execution",
                         "physical-rw", "msr-rw", "token-steal", "ppl-bypass",
                         "process-control") for p in prims):
                dangerous_ioctls.add(code)

        # BFS from each non-dangerous IOCTL to find paths to dangerous ones
        attack_sequences: List[dict] = []
        for start in graph:
            if start in dangerous_ioctls:
                continue  # Already dangerous directly
            # BFS
            visited = {start}
            queue = [(start, [start])]
            while queue:
                current, path = queue.pop(0)
                for nxt in graph.get(current, []):
                    if nxt in visited:
                        continue
                    visited.add(nxt)
                    new_path = path + [nxt]
                    if nxt in dangerous_ioctls:
                        prims = self.ioctl_primitives.get(nxt, [])
                        attack_sequences.append({
                            "sequence": new_path,
                            "target": nxt,
                            "primitives": prims,
                            "length": len(new_path),
                        })
                    elif len(new_path) < 5:
                        queue.append((nxt, new_path))

        # Phase 4: Report state transitions
        self.state_transitions = transitions
        self.attack_sequences = attack_sequences

        self.findings.append(Finding(
            title=f"State machine: {len(transitions)} cross-IOCTL dependencies found",
            severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
            description=f"Analysis of global variable access patterns reveals "
                        f"{len(transitions)} state dependencies between IOCTL handlers. "
                        f"One IOCTL writes a global that another IOCTL reads, creating "
                        f"an implicit state machine.\n\n"
                        f"Dependencies:\n" + "\n".join(
                            f"  {decode_ioctl(t['from'])['code']} → "
                            f"{decode_ioctl(t['to'])['code']} "
                            f"({t['count']} shared global(s) at "
                            f"{', '.join(f'0x{g:X}' for g in t['shared_globals'][:3])})"
                            for t in transitions[:10]
                        ),
            location="Cross-handler analysis",
            details={
                "transition_count": str(len(transitions)),
                "transitions": [
                    {"from": f"0x{t['from']:08X}", "to": f"0x{t['to']:08X}",
                     "shared": len(t["shared_globals"])}
                    for t in transitions
                ],
            },
        ))

        # Report attack sequences
        for seq in attack_sequences:
            path_str = " → ".join(decode_ioctl(c)["code"] for c in seq["sequence"])
            prims = ", ".join(seq["primitives"])
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title=f"Multi-step attack: {path_str} → [{prims}]",
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                description=f"A {seq['length']}-step IOCTL sequence unlocks dangerous "
                            f"primitives. The attacker must send IOCTLs in this order:\n\n"
                            f"  {path_str}\n\n"
                            f"The final IOCTL ({decode_ioctl(seq['target'])['code']}) "
                            f"provides: {prims}.\n"
                            f"Earlier IOCTLs set required global state variables that "
                            f"gate access to the dangerous operation.",
                location="State Machine Analysis",
                ioctl_code=seq["target"],
                details={
                    "sequence": [f"0x{c:08X}" for c in seq["sequence"]],
                    "target_primitives": seq["primitives"],
                    "steps": str(seq["length"]),
                },
            ))

        if attack_sequences:
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title=f"Total multi-step attack sequences: {len(attack_sequences)}",
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                description=f"Found {len(attack_sequences)} multi-step attack sequence(s) "
                            f"where sending IOCTLs in a specific order unlocks dangerous "
                            f"primitives. Single-IOCTL testing will MISS these vulnerabilities.",
                location="State Machine Analysis",
                details={"sequence_count": str(len(attack_sequences))},
            ))

    # ── Cross-IOCTL dataflow typing ───────────────────────────────────────
    # An exploit chain is only weaponizable if the value an "earlier" IOCTL
    # produces matches a type the "later" IOCTL consumes. We type each
    # IOCTL's inputs (from recovered struct field types) and outputs (from
    # the primitive labels + behavior APIs), then for every chain we scan
    # all (earlier_prim, later_prim) pairs and emit a typed edge whenever
    # an output type intersects an input type.
    #
    # Edge type vocabulary (intentionally small):
    #   kernel-address, user-address, physical-address, handle, peprocess,
    #   pid, tid, size, msr-index, msr-value, arbitrary-data
    _PRIM_OUTPUT_TYPES = {
        "arbitrary-read":  {"kernel-address", "arbitrary-data"},
        "info-leak":       {"kernel-address"},
        "physical-rw":     {"physical-address"},
        "msr-rw":          {"msr-value"},
        "process-control": {"peprocess", "handle"},
        "process-attach":  {"peprocess"},
    }
    _INPUT_FIELD_TYPE_MAP = {
        "ADDRESS":          "kernel-address",
        "BUFFER_PTR":       "user-address",
        "PHYSICAL_ADDRESS": "physical-address",
        "HANDLE":           "handle",
        "PHANDLE":          "handle",
        "PEPROCESS":        "peprocess",
        "PID":              "pid",
        "TID":              "tid",
        "SIZE":             "size",
        "MSR_INDEX":        "msr-index",
    }

    def _ioctl_input_types(self, code: int) -> set:
        """Set of edge-type labels this IOCTL consumes from the user buffer."""
        out: set = set()
        for fld in self.ioctl_structs.get(code, []):
            ft = fld.get("field_type")
            mapped = self._INPUT_FIELD_TYPE_MAP.get(ft)
            if mapped:
                out.add(mapped)
        return out

    def _ioctl_output_types(self, code: int) -> set:
        """Set of edge-type labels this IOCTL produces back to user-mode.
        Inferred from the IOCTL's primitive labels (cheap, conservative)."""
        out: set = set()
        for prim in self.ioctl_primitives.get(code, []):
            out |= self._PRIM_OUTPUT_TYPES.get(prim, set())
        return out

    def type_chain_dataflow(self):
        """Decorate each exploit chain with typed edges between IOCTLs.

        For every (prim_a, prim_b) pair drawn from chain['primitives'] in
        order, find IOCTLs that match each side. If an output type of an
        earlier-side IOCTL intersects an input type of a later-side IOCTL,
        emit one edge — the chain is genuinely weaponizable.

        A chain with zero typed edges is still listed by build_exploit_chains
        as a "primitive available" template, but the absence of edges tells
        the operator the chain needs an external info-leak / addr source.
        """
        if not getattr(self, "exploit_chains", None):
            return
        # IOCTL by primitive
        by_prim: dict = {}
        for code, prims in self.ioctl_primitives.items():
            for p in prims:
                by_prim.setdefault(p, []).append(code)

        for chain in self.exploit_chains:
            edges: list = []
            prims = chain.get("primitives") or []
            for i, p_a in enumerate(prims):
                for p_b in prims[i + 1:]:
                    src_codes = by_prim.get(p_a, [])
                    dst_codes = by_prim.get(p_b, [])
                    for sc in src_codes:
                        outs = self._ioctl_output_types(sc)
                        if not outs:
                            continue
                        for dc in dst_codes:
                            if sc == dc:
                                continue
                            ins = self._ioctl_input_types(dc)
                            common = outs & ins
                            for typ in sorted(common):
                                edges.append({
                                    "from":      f"0x{sc:08X}",
                                    "from_prim": p_a,
                                    "to":        f"0x{dc:08X}",
                                    "to_prim":   p_b,
                                    "type":      typ,
                                })
            # Dedup edges (same from→to→type)
            seen = set()
            unique_edges = []
            for e in edges:
                k = (e["from"], e["to"], e["type"])
                if k not in seen:
                    seen.add(k)
                    unique_edges.append(e)
            chain["dataflow_edges"] = unique_edges

```

`drivertool/scanner/imports.py`:

```py
"""Import and compiler security scanning."""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from drivertool.constants import DANGEROUS_IMPORTS, Severity
from drivertool.models import Finding

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    pass

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class ImportScanMixin:
    """Mixin: scan_imports, scan_compiler_security"""

    def scan_imports(self):
        all_imports = []
        for dll, funcs in self.pe.imports.items():
            for func in funcs:
                all_imports.append(func)
                if func in DANGEROUS_IMPORTS:
                    sev, desc, hint = DANGEROUS_IMPORTS[func]
                    if sev <= Severity.INFO:
                        continue
                    self.findings.append(Finding(
                        title=f"Dangerous import: {func}",
                        severity=sev,
                        description=desc,
                        location=f"Import Table ({dll})",
                        poc_hint=hint if hint else None,
                        details={"dll": dll, "function": func},
                    ))

        # Check for positive indicators
        has_probe_read = "ProbeForRead" in all_imports
        has_probe_write = "ProbeForWrite" in all_imports
        if not has_probe_read and not has_probe_write:
            dangerous_count = sum(1 for f in all_imports if f in DANGEROUS_IMPORTS
                                  and DANGEROUS_IMPORTS[f][0] >= Severity.HIGH)
            if dangerous_count > 0:
                self.findings.append(Finding(
                    title="No ProbeForRead/ProbeForWrite imports found",
                    severity=Severity.HIGH,
                    description="Driver imports dangerous APIs but never imports "
                                "ProbeForRead or ProbeForWrite. User buffer validation "
                                "is likely missing.",
                    location="Import Table",
                ))

    def scan_compiler_security(self):
        """BinSkim-style check for missing binary security mitigations."""
        feats = getattr(self.pe, "security_flags", None) or self.pe._parse_security_features()
        CHECKS = [
            ("NX_COMPAT",       "DEP/NX (/NXCOMPAT)",
             "Executable pages usable for shellcode without ROP chains."),
            ("DYNAMIC_BASE",    "ASLR (/DYNAMICBASE)",
             "Driver loads at predictable address — KASLR bypass trivial."),
            ("FORCE_INTEGRITY", "Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY)",
             "Driver can load without a valid Authenticode signature."),
            ("GUARD_CF",        "Control Flow Guard (/guard:cf)",
             "Indirect calls unvalidated — control-flow hijacking easier."),
            ("GS_COOKIE",       "Stack canary (/GS)",
             "Stack overflows not detected by compiler canary check."),
            ("HIGH_ENTROPY_VA", "High-entropy ASLR (/HIGHENTROPYVA)",
             "64-bit ASLR entropy reduced — address brute-force more feasible."),
        ]
        for key, name, risk in CHECKS:
            if not feats.get(key, False):
                self.findings.append(Finding(
                    title=f"Missing mitigation: {name}",
                    severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
                    description=f"{name} is not enabled. {risk}",
                    location="PE DllCharacteristics / LoadConfig",
                    details={"mitigation": name, "flag": key},
                ))
        if feats.get("HVCI_COMPATIBLE"):
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title="Driver is HVCI-compatible",
                severity=Severity.INFO,
                description="FORCE_INTEGRITY set and no W+X sections. "
                            "Meets basic HVCI (Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity) requirements.",
                location="PE headers",
                details={"hvci": "compatible"},
            ))
        else:
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title="Driver is NOT HVCI-compatible",
                severity=Severity.HIGH,
                description="Driver fails HVCI checks (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY or has W+X sections). "
                            "Cannot load in HVCI-enabled environments without policy bypass.",
                location="PE headers",
                details={"hvci": "incompatible"},
            ))

```

`drivertool/scanner/ioctl_scan.py`:

```py
"""IOCTL handler analysis and structure reconstruction."""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import struct
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import capstone.x86_const as x86c

from drivertool.constants import (
    DANGEROUS_IMPORTS, IRP_MJ_INTERESTING, IRP_MJ_NAMES, Severity,
)
from drivertool.models import Finding
from drivertool.ioctl import (
    HANDLER_PURPOSE_MAP, IOCTL_METHOD_LABEL,
    decode_ioctl, is_valid_ioctl,
)
from drivertool.ioctl_cfg import IOCTLDispatchCFG
from drivertool.dispatch_finder import DispatcherFinder
from drivertool.handler_emulator import emulate_handler
from drivertool.taint import TaintTracker

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    pass

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class IOCTLScanMixin:
    """Mixin for IOCTL scanning and analysis."""

    _API_BEHAVIOR = {
        # Memory operations
        "MmMapIoSpace": ("MEMORY", "Map physical address to virtual kernel address"),
        "MmMapIoSpaceEx": ("MEMORY", "Map physical address to virtual kernel address (extended)"),
        "MmMapLockedPages": ("MEMORY", "Map locked pages into address space"),
        "MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache": ("MEMORY", "Map locked pages with cache type"),
        "MmUnmapIoSpace": ("MEMORY", "Unmap previously mapped physical memory"),
        "MmUnmapLockedPages": ("MEMORY", "Unmap previously locked pages"),
        "MmCopyVirtualMemory": ("MEMORY", "Copy between virtual address spaces (cross-process)"),
        "MmCopyMemory": ("MEMORY", "Copy from physical or virtual memory"),
        "MmGetPhysicalAddress": ("MEMORY", "Translate virtual to physical address"),
        "MmAllocateContiguousMemory": ("MEMORY", "Allocate contiguous physical memory"),

    def scan_ioctl_handler(self):
        ep_addr, ep_bytes = self.pe.get_entry_point_bytes(count=4096)
        image_base = self.pe.pe.OPTIONAL_HEADER.ImageBase

        # ── Extract all MajorFunction slots from DriverEntry ──────────────
        major_funcs: Dict[int, int] = {}
        if ep_bytes:
            major_funcs = self.dis.extract_major_functions(
                ep_bytes, ep_addr, image_base)

        if not major_funcs:
            # Try WDF detection before falling back to brute-force
            if self._try_wdf_dispatch():
                return

            # Fallback 2: scan all code sections for dispatcher signatures
            # (jump tables, cmp chains, IOS field loads)
            finder = DispatcherFinder(self.pe, self.dis)
            candidates = finder.find_candidates()
            dispatch_found = False
            for disp_va, confidence in candidates[:3]:
                logger.debug(
                    "DispatcherFinder candidate: 0x%X (confidence %.1f)",
                    disp_va, confidence)
                cfg = IOCTLDispatchCFG(self.pe, self.dis)
                ioctl_map = cfg.reconstruct(disp_va)
                if ioctl_map and len(ioctl_map) >= 2:
                    self._analyze_from_ioctl_map(ioctl_map, disp_va, slot=0x0E)
                    self._try_reverse_hash_dispatch(
                        list(ioctl_map.keys()), slot=0x0E)
                    dispatch_found = True
                    # Don't return — merge with bruteforce to catch any
                    # out-of-line or non-table IOCTLs the driver may also use.
                    break

            # Fallback 3: pure brute-force constant scan — ALWAYS run so we
            # don't miss IOCTLs that DispatcherFinder / IOCTLDispatchCFG
            # failed to recover.
            self._scan_ioctl_codes_bruteforce()
            self._resolve_bruteforce_purposes()
            return

        # ── Report all interesting slots ──────────────────────────────────
        for slot, hva in sorted(major_funcs.items()):
            slot_name = IRP_MJ_NAMES.get(slot, f"IRP_MJ_slot_{slot:#x}")
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title=f"{slot_name} handler at 0x{hva:X}",
                severity=Severity.INFO,
                description=f"MajorFunction[{slot:#x}] ({slot_name}) set in DriverEntry",
                location=f"0x{hva:X}",
                details={"slot": f"{slot:#x}", "slot_name": slot_name,
                         "handler": f"0x{hva:X}"},
            ))

        # ── Analyse IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL and other interesting slots ─────
        for slot in sorted(major_funcs):
            if slot not in IRP_MJ_INTERESTING:
                continue
            hva = major_funcs[slot]
            slot_name = IRP_MJ_NAMES.get(slot, f"slot_{slot:#x}")

            if slot in (0x0D, 0x0E, 0x0F):
                # DEVICE_CONTROL / INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL / FS_CONTROL
                # — full IOCTL CFG reconstruction. Slot is carried forward so
                # codes can be tagged FSCTL vs IOCTL in the output.
                cfg = IOCTLDispatchCFG(self.pe, self.dis)
                ioctl_map = cfg.reconstruct(hva)
                if ioctl_map:
                    self._analyze_from_ioctl_map(ioctl_map, hva, slot=slot)
                    # Hash-dispatch reversal — if the discovered constants
                    # don't look like real CTL_CODEs, the dispatcher may be
                    # comparing hashes. Try to invert them.
                    self._try_reverse_hash_dispatch(
                        list(ioctl_map.keys()), slot=slot)
                else:
                    rva = hva - image_base
                    data = self.pe.get_bytes_at_rva(rva, 16384)
                    if data:
                        insns = self.dis.disassemble_function(
                            data, hva, max_insns=1200)
                        self._analyze_ioctl_handler(insns, hva, slot=slot)
            else:
                # READ/WRITE/CREATE — scan the handler body for dangerous calls
                self._analyze_irp_handler(hva, slot_name)

    def _analyze_ioctl_handler(self, insns: list, base: int, slot: int = 0x0E):
        ioctl_codes = []
        has_probe = False

        # Resolve call targets to import names
        call_targets = self.dis.find_all_call_targets(insns)
        called_funcs = set()
        for _, target in call_targets:
            if target in self.pe.iat_map:
                called_funcs.add(self.pe.iat_map[target])

        if "ProbeForRead" in called_funcs or "ProbeForWrite" in called_funcs:
            has_probe = True

        # Extract IOCTL codes from CMP instructions
        # Also look for the conditional jump right after each CMP to find the handler VA
        for i, insn in enumerate(insns):
            if insn.mnemonic in ("cmp", "sub") and len(insn.operands) == 2:
                op = insn.operands[1]
                if op.type == x86c.X86_OP_IMM:
                    imm = op.imm & 0xFFFFFFFF
                    if 0x80000 <= imm <= 0xFFFFFFFF and (imm & 0x3) <= 3:
                        device_type = (imm >> 16) & 0xFFFF
                        if device_type > 0:
                            # Look ahead up to 4 instructions for JE/JZ to the handler
                            handler_va = None
                            for j in range(i + 1, min(i + 5, len(insns))):
                                ji = insns[j]
                                if ji.mnemonic in ("je", "jz") and ji.operands:
                                    jop = ji.operands[0]
                                    if jop.type == x86c.X86_OP_IMM:
                                        handler_va = jop.imm
                                        break
                                # Stop at another CMP or unconditional JMP
                                if ji.mnemonic in ("cmp", "jmp"):
                                    break
                            ioctl_codes.append((insn.address, imm, handler_va))

        # Build handler VA bounds for purpose detection
        handler_vas_sorted = sorted(set(
            hva for _, _, hva in ioctl_codes if hva is not None))
        handler_bounds: Dict[int, int] = {}
        for idx, hva in enumerate(handler_vas_sorted):
            if idx + 1 < len(handler_vas_sorted):
                nxt = handler_vas_sorted[idx + 1]
                if 0 < (nxt - hva) < 0x400:
                    handler_bounds[hva] = nxt

        for addr, code, handler_va in ioctl_codes:
            decoded = decode_ioctl(code)
            method = code & 0x3
            # Track all real IOCTL codes found
            if code not in self.ioctl_codes:
                self.ioctl_codes.append(code)
            self.ioctl_origin_slot[code] = slot
            # Try to determine what the handler does
            if handler_va and code not in self.ioctl_purposes:
                ma = handler_bounds.get(handler_va, 0)
                purpose = self._get_ioctl_purpose(handler_va, max_addr=ma)
                if purpose:
                    self.ioctl_purposes[code] = purpose

            if method == 3:  # METHOD_NEITHER
                self.findings.append(Finding(
                    title=f"IOCTL {decoded['code']} uses METHOD_NEITHER",
                    severity=Severity.CRITICAL if not has_probe else Severity.HIGH,
                    description="METHOD_NEITHER passes raw user-mode pointers to the driver. "
                                "Without ProbeForRead/ProbeForWrite, this enables arbitrary "
                                "kernel read/write from usermode.",
                    location=f"0x{addr:X}",
                    poc_hint="ioctl_method_neither",
                    ioctl_code=code,
                    details={
                        "ioctl_code": decoded["code"],
                        "method": "METHOD_NEITHER",
                        "device_type": f"0x{decoded['device_type']:X}",
                        "function": f"0x{decoded['function']:X}",
                        "has_probe": str(has_probe),
                        **({"handler": f"0x{handler_va:X}"} if handler_va else {}),
                    },
                ))
            else:
                self.findings.append(Finding(
                    title=f"IOCTL {decoded['code']} ({decoded['method_name']})",
                    severity=Severity.INFO,
                    description=f"IOCTL handler processes code {decoded['code']}",
                    location=f"0x{addr:X}",
                    poc_hint="ioctl_generic",
                    ioctl_code=code,
                    details={
                        "ioctl_code": decoded["code"],
                        "method": decoded["method_name"],
                        **({"handler": f"0x{handler_va:X}"} if handler_va else {}),
                    },
                ))

        if ioctl_codes and not has_probe:
            self.findings.append(Finding(
                title="IOCTL handler lacks ProbeForRead/ProbeForWrite",
                severity=Severity.HIGH,
                description="No calls to ProbeForRead or ProbeForWrite found in "
                            "the IOCTL handler. User buffer access may be unvalidated.",
                location=f"Handler at 0x{base:X}",
                poc_hint="ioctl_no_probe",
            ))

    def _try_reverse_hash_dispatch(self, candidate_codes: List[int],
                                    slot: int = 0x0E) -> None:
        """If the dispatcher's switch constants look like hash values
        rather than real CTL_CODEs, brute-force candidate IOCTL codes
        through known hash functions to recover the originals.

        Results are appended to ``self.ioctl_codes``,
        ``self.ioctl_origin_slot`` and ``self.hash_dispatch_codes``.
        """
        from drivertool import hash_dispatch as _hd
        if not candidate_codes or not _hd.looks_like_hashes(candidate_codes):
            return
```
